reinforced_signals.theme: Iran-Middle East Conflict Escalation & NATO Involvement
wf1_signals: Middle East Escalation Day 6: NATO Intercepts Iranian Missile Targeting Turkey
wf4_signals: NATO Intercepts Iranian Ballistic Missile Headed for Turkey
U.S. and Israel Strike Iran Overnight
reinforced_signals.notes: Triple-confirmed across WF1 (general media), WF3 (Korean domestic perspective), WF4 (global multilingual sources). Each workflow adds unique dimensions: WF1 emphasizes AI-military nexus, WF3 focuses on Korean Peninsula security implications, WF4 provides global alliance-level analysis.

reinforced_signals.theme: Hormuz Strait Blockade & Energy Supply Crisis
wf3_signals: Hormuz Strait Completely Blocked: 50/day to Zero
reinforced_signals.notes: WF3 reports complete blockade (50 to 0 tankers); WF4 reports 90% reduction with $29M charter costs. WF4 uniquely captures Qatar LNG halt and compound multi-source energy crisis. Korean-specific impacts (7 trapped tankers, 70% import dependency) from WF3.

wf1_signals: Claude Used for Military Target Identification
Pentagon Designates Anthropic as Supply-Chain Risk
wf3_signals: Anthropic Expelled from US Defense Contracts
reinforced_signals.notes: WF1 provides most detailed coverage of AI-military nexus. WF3 adds Korean industry perspective on AI bifurcation. WF2 academic perspective on AI safety arms race provides theoretical foundation.
Both WF1 and WF3 independently report GPT-5.4. WF2 academic research on LLM safety (refusal ablation) provides scientific context for capability advancement risks.

wf3_signals: KOSPI Extreme Volatility -- Multiple Sidecar Triggers
Michael Burry warns 'signs of the end'
reinforced_signals.notes: WF3 captures retail investor perspective and sequential sidecar triggers. WF4 captures circuit breaker activation and government emergency response. Combined paints complete picture of Korean financial market stress.

reinforced_signals.theme: China Strategic Resource Hoarding & Economic Slowdown
reinforced_signals.notes: Multilingual sourcing (Caixin, Pengpai in Chinese; SCMP in English) captures signals not visible in English-only or Korean-only sources.

contradictions.tension: WF3 reports 'complete blockade' (50 to 0 tankers, 100% reduction) while WF4 reports '90% traffic drop' (not complete). This discrepancy may reflect Korean media framing vs. international reporting precision.
contradictions.resolution: WF4's $29M charter cost data and WSJ sourcing suggest the more nuanced '90% reduction' figure may be more precise, though both agree on severe disruption.

contradictions.tension: WF1 reports simultaneous military USE of Claude for targeting AND supply-chain risk designation. WF3 reports outright EXPULSION from defense contracts. These may represent different stages or aspects of the same process.
contradictions.resolution: Both may be accurate -- operational units may continue using Claude while procurement policy excludes Anthropic from new contracts.
